relevant_files.relevance: Exposes typed clientWs, terminal, snapshot, workspaceRuntime, and wsTransportMode runtime limits.
Classifies input sends into ok, client-backpressure, and client-hard-backpressure using bufferedAmount and UTF-8 payload bytes.

relevant_files.signature: send path backpressure and transport metadata
relevant_files.relevance: Applies input-only browser bufferedAmount gating and reports low-water recovery metadata.

relevant_files.signature: bounded transport outbox and input barrier integration
relevant_files.relevance: Queues backpressured input, enforces TTL/byte limits, and clears barrier only after low-water recovery.
Bounds visible terminal output, preserves callback order, avoids code point splits, and reports overflow.
Routes visible live output through the scheduler and recovery path instead of direct unbounded term.write.

relevant_files.signature: terminal snapshot storage budget and tombstone handling
relevant_files.relevance: Applies runtime per-snapshot cap, total budget, max entries, and tombstone TTL.
Computes resident terminal runtimes using visible pinning, hidden TTL, workspace cap, terminal cap, and disconnected exclusion.
Parses unified, split-shadow, and split websocket channel requests.
Creates split control/output connection metadata, pair tokens, and logical client groups.
Reroutes output failure to control, gates recovery to full split mode, and preserves replay/repair pending output for fallback recovery snapshots.

relevant_files.signature: terminal payload routing and transport queue
relevant_files.relevance: Routes terminal payloads to output socket in split mode and bounds server-side output queues.

missing_evidence.reason: The completion report intentionally leaves non-2002 live validation and long-duration soak as operational validation work.
